domain: Maasai engang/boma concentric thorn-ring settlement (innermost cattle corral + middle huts + outer thorn fence; concentric priority-by-distance architecture)
specific_mechanism: Three-ring KV-cache hierarchy organized by attention-importance percentile rather than recency: (i) inner ring = top-K most-attended KVs across recent window (never evicted), (ii) middle ring = mid-percentile KVs (evictable on miss), (iii) outer ring = fixed safety/system-prompt tokens (last access, always preserved as a defensive boundary). Access order is inner → middle → outer.
llm_application: BOMA-RING-KV: a 3-tier concentric KV-cache memory hierarchy organized by attention-importance and role rather than recency. Inner ring R_in stores the top-K_in KVs by cumulative attention score (the cattle, highest-value, never evicted); middle ring R_mid stores mid-percentile KVs (the huts, evictable on miss); outer ring R_out stores fixed system-prompt/safety/retrieval-guard tokens (the thorn fence, defensive boundary, accessed last). Retrieval traverses R_in→R_mid→R_out with early exit if confidence sufficient. Differs from LMCache (hot/warm/cold by recency, not attention-importance percentile), IMPRESS (importance-aware single-tier prefix, not concentric ring), CTkvr (centroid-then-token retrieval, not value-tier traversal), Google Tiered KV (latency-bandwidth tier, not value-priority). Differs from R408 ICELANDIC-CHAIN (provenance audit chain, not value-priority ring).
novelty_claim: LMCache + IMPRESS + CTkvr + Google Tiered KV + KVCache-Centric Memory cover hierarchical/multi-tier KV cache space. BOMA-RING-KV adds explicit (i) concentric-ring + (ii) safety-boundary outer-ring + (iii) value-percentile inner-ring + (iv) inner-to-outer traversal order. Recombination.
